sections.question:
s.
Please contact me it you wish additional information.
sections.conclusion: Michael Bennett, Executive Director, provided point-by-point responses: (1) FPPC retains only reports and statements required by Government Code Section 81003, which must remain open for public inspection; (2) enclosed a copy of the statement attached to forms; (3) assigned a full-time Documents Examiner to maintain accurate, complete, and timely files; (4) record transfers under the Executive Order are not a concern since all relevant records are public; (5) no increase in requests to view files; (6) no requests to amend files received; (7) not applicable; (8) Executive Order has not had significant impact; (9) workload has not increased; (10) no public questions received; (11) no changes to record keeping procedures.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti inquired about the FPPC's compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, requesting information on records jurisdiction, form statements, file maintenance procedures, information transfer compatibility determinations, and the order's operational impact on the agency.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed full compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, stating that records are limited to those required by statute and are open to public inspection, a Documents Examiner has been assigned to ensure file quality, and the order has had no significant operational impact on the agency.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some formatting issues but readable. Includes both the incoming request (dated January 19, 1977) and the FPPC response (dated March 1, 1977). No distinct 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but questions and answers are clearly demarcated by numbering. Response addresses each question sequentially.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: s.
Please contact me it you wish additional information.

CONCLUSION: Michael Bennett, Executive Director, provided point-by-point responses: (1) FPPC retains only reports and statements required by Government Code Section 81003, which must remain open for public inspection; (2) enclosed a copy of the statement attached to forms; (3) assigned a full-time Documents Examiner to maintain accurate, complete, and timely files; (4) record transfers under the Executive Order are not a concern since all relevant records are public; (5) no increase in requests to view files; (6) no requests to amend files received; (7) not applicable; (8) Executive Order has not had significant impact; (9) workload has not increased; (10) no public questions received; (11) no changes to record keeping procedures.

embedding.summary: On March 1, 1977, the FPPC Executive Director responded to a detailed inquiry from Senator David A. Roberti regarding the Commission's compliance with Executive Order B-22-76, addressing 11 specific questions about records management, information collection, file maintenance, and the order's agency impact.
